channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Tower 16 for Mac – Introducing AI Commits ✨
item.description: Writing commit messages pulls you out of your flow. You've just finished implementing something, your head is full of context, and now you have to stop and summarize it in a way your future self...

item.title: Checkpoint da Série: o que já funciona, o que quebrou e para onde vamos (Parte 4.5)
item.description: Quatro posts, três plataformas, um bug encontrado em produção. Antes de seguir para App Links e Universal Links no ar de verdade — com domínio, verificação bidirecional e os problemas que só...

item.title: Building a 3-Class Lung Cancer Image Classifier with TensorFlow and Flask
item.description: Medical imaging is one of the most rewarding spaces to apply deep learning. Pathologists spend years learning to distinguish subtle visual patterns in tissue samples, and even then, fatigue and...

item.title: Leaked images reveal a dual-lens pro version of DJI’s next Osmo Pocket camera
item.description: Reliable leaker Igor Bogdanov shared an image on X yesterday of an unseen person holding two versions of a handheld stabilized camera, including one that's rumored to be a pro version of the DJI...

item.title: Building a Voice-Controlled AI Agent using AssemblyAI and Groq
item.description: Introduction In this project, I built a voice-controlled AI agent that converts spoken commands into executable actions such as generating code, creating files, and summarizing text. The goal was...

item.title: 🚀 I Built TechieLearn — An AI-Powered Learning Platform for Developers
item.description: Hey devs 👋 After ~6 months of building, iterating, and rethinking how we actually learn tech… I finally shipped something I personally needed. TechieLearn — an AI-powered learning platform focused...

item.title: Are Book Summaries Worth It?
item.description: TL;DR Every now and then, a “shortcut” to learning shows up and promises to save us time, money, and maybe even a bit of guilt about the unread books piling up on our shelves. Yup, guilty as...

item.title: What Is the Best Website for Exchange Rates? (2026 Guide)
item.description: If you search "best website for exchange rates," you get a dozen contradictory answers — Google Finance, XE, Wise, Reuters, your bank's app. They all show a number next to a currency pair, and...

item.title: Bugmageddon Is Real. But Bug Discovery Isn’t the Real Bottleneck Anymore.
item.description: The scary part about the new "Bugmageddon" story is not that AI can find vulnerabilities faster. That part was inevitable. The real shift is that bug discovery is getting cheap. And once that...

item.title: docker40
item.description: - name: "DEBUG: Dump all Ansible variables" ansible.builtin.debug: msg: "{{ hostvars[inventory_hostname] }}" ignore_errors: yes

item.title: The Most Popular from Q1 2026
item.description: Every quarter, dev.to surfaces thousands of posts. Most are good. Some hit a nerve. Here are the ones from Q1 2026 that got the biggest response. The Big News A New Chapter: DEV is Joining Forces...

item.title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
item.description: Human trials on the hybrid sensor are expected to begin in the years ahead.

item.title: Google’s Spotlight-like desktop search bar for Windows is available for everyone
item.description: Last year, Google announced that it was testing a Google desktop app for Windows that resembles macOS's Spotlight feature, and now the app is available globally in English. You can download the...

item.title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
item.description: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

item.title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
item.description: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

item.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
item.description: Video showing Skills in Chrome

item.title: Chrome now lets you turn AI prompts into repeatable ‘Skills’
item.description: Google is launching a new Chrome workflow feature that allows you to reuse your favorite Gemini commands across multiple webpages. Any AI prompts can now be saved as "Skills" in the Chrome desktop...

item.title: The world’s oldest art, now in 6K IMAX
item.description: The earliest paintings were made over 32,000 years ago - the very first forms of art and culture. They weren't discovered until 1994, when cave explorers in France stumbled into the Chauvet Cave....

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m speaking at DemocracyXChange 2026 in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on April 18, 2026. I’m speaking at the SANS AI Cybersecurity...

item.title: Sony’s new 1440p OLED gaming monitor seems a lot better than its first
item.description: The original InZone M10S OLED gaming monitor stood out for a mix of reasons when I reviewed it for Polygon back in late 2024 - some good (impressively small stand, high 480Hz refresh rate) and...

item.title: Sony’s latest gaming headset offers great open-back audio
item.description: Sony's PC-focused InZone brand is covering a lot of ground, now adding open-back headsets to its growing catalog of gaming accessories. The $199.99 H6 Air borrows the comfortable, easy-to-adjust...

item.title: New PHP Composer Flaws Enable Arbitrary Command Execution — Patches Released
item.description: Two high-severity security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in Composer, a package manager for PHP, that, if successfully exploited, could result in arbitrary command execution. The...

item.title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it. There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

item.title: DaVinci Resolve adds new photo editing tools to take on Lightroom and Photoshop
item.description: Blackmagic Design announced an update to its powerful multipurpose post-production software today adding new tools and improved workflows for editing photos. In addition to helping position...

item.title: Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.

item.title: Bose’s noise-crushing QC Ultra Earbuds are nearly 20 percent off right now
item.description: When it comes to the best noise-canceling earbuds, the competition has gotten fierce. Apple, Sony, Samsung, and Google all offer excellent options, though, unsurprisingly, Bose still retains the...

item.title: Google Adds Rust-Based DNS Parser into Pixel 10 Modem to Enhance Security
item.description: Google has announced the integration of a Rust-based Domain Name System (DNS) parser into the modem firmware as part of its ongoing efforts to beef up the security of Pixel devices and push...

item.title: Google brings its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature to India
item.description: The feature lets users connect Google accounts like Gmail and Photos to get personalized answers.

item.title: AI-Driven Pushpaganda Scam Exploits Google Discover to Spread Scareware and Ad Fraud
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have unmasked a novel ad fraud scheme that has been found to leverage search engine poisoning (SEO) techniques and artificial intelligence (AI)-generated content to push...

item.title: How vibe-coding app Anything is rebuilding after getting booted from the App Store twice
item.description: Anything is planning to launch a desktop companion app to aid mobile app development after its App Store removal.
